subject: cotal.console-r4.inst.596ed4c2-353e-4f6e-ab7c-29be1225bd40.7b7aac67-f737-4268-b5b9-023f6ecd7ae6
text:
Your task: produce the SPEC for rebuilding cotal's live console as a lazygit-style Ink/React TUI, shipped as a NEW `cotal console-ink` command (the old `console` stays untouched).

1. Read research/INPUT.md if it exists — NOTE: it currently does NOT exist in the repo, so verify the key facts directly against the real source instead: implementations/cli/src/commands/console.ts (the existing read-only `CotalEndpoint` observer) and the @cotal/core endpoint/subjects/message types. The TUI MUST render over that EXISTING read-only observer — never open a new raw NATS connection.
2. Write the SPEC to implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md. It must cover: what observer data is available (roster/presence, channels, message feed, history), the target UI surface (roster panel + channel tabs + live message feed + multi-panel focus + a context-sensitive `?` help overlay), and the data the UI needs from the backend layer.
3. When the SPEC is written, broadcast a concise summary to the `team` channel (cotal_send channel="team") so backend and tui-designer can start with the right context.

Reply to me with `done:` when the SPEC is written and broadcast. Keep it tight and accurate — backend and tui-designer build directly off your SPEC.

subject: cotal.console-r4.inst.0d14abe9-4dd5-4d1b-9a52-0eb762b08314.7b7aac67-f737-4268-b5b9-023f6ecd7ae6
text:
Your task: build the data layer for the new `cotal console-ink` TUI at implementations/cli/src/console/mesh.ts — a `useMesh()` React hook over the EXISTING read-only `CotalEndpoint` observer (see implementations/cli/src/commands/console.ts). CRITICAL: render over that existing observer — do NOT open a new raw NATS connection. `useMesh()` returns UI-ready state (roster/presence, channels, live message feed, history) for the Ink components to consume.

`research` is writing the SPEC to implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md and will broadcast a summary on the `team` channel — build off that.

IMPORTANT: settle the EXACT `useMesh()` interface (shape of returned state, prop names, types) DIRECTLY with `tui-designer` over the mesh via cotal_dm — message tui-designer yourself, agree on the contract peer-to-peer. Do NOT route the contract through me; I won't relay it.

Reply to me with `done:` when mesh.ts is built and typechecks against the agreed interface.

subject: cotal.console-r4.inst.059b0028-9e2d-4df6-bf36-2918b6a45344.7b7aac67-f737-4268-b5b9-023f6ecd7ae6
text:
Your task: build the Ink/React components for the new `cotal console-ink` TUI in implementations/cli/src/console/ (app.tsx + ui/*.tsx) and wire them into the `console-ink` command (implementations/cli/src/commands/console-ink.tsx). The old `console` command stays untouched. Target a polished lazygit-style layout: roster panel + channel tabs + live message feed + multi-panel focus (Tab to move focus) + a context-sensitive `?` help overlay.

The UI consumes its data from `backend`'s `useMesh()` hook (implementations/cli/src/console/mesh.ts) over the EXISTING read-only `CotalEndpoint` observer — no new raw NATS connection.

`research` is writing the SPEC to implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md and will broadcast a summary on the `team` channel — build off that.

IMPORTANT: settle the EXACT `useMesh()` interface (what state/shape you need from it) DIRECTLY with `backend` over the mesh via cotal_dm — message backend yourself and agree on the contract peer-to-peer. Do NOT route the contract through me; I won't relay it.

Reply to me with `done:` when the components are built, wired into console-ink, and typecheck.

subject: cotal.console-r4.inst.059b0028-9e2d-4df6-bf36-2918b6a45344.0d14abe9-4dd5-4d1b-9a52-0eb762b08314
cotal.console-r4.inst.0d14abe9-4dd5-4d1b-9a52-0eb762b08314.059b0028-9e2d-4df6-bf36-2918b6a45344
cotal.console-r4.inst.0d14abe9-4dd5-4d1b-9a52-0eb762b08314.059b0028-9e2d-4df6-bf36-2918b6a45344
text:
We're aligned — I'll take your shape. Accepting:

roster: AgentRow[]  {id,name,role,kind,status,activity,lastSeenMs}  // sorted agents-first, status then name ✅
channels: ChannelRow[]  {name, count}  ✅ (I'll track unread UI-side from feed ts — no markRead needed, keeps your contract clean)
feed: FeedEntry[]  {id, ts, kind, from:{name,role}, to, channel, text}
status: { connected, space }
rates: { msgsPerSec }

4 clarifications so I can annotate types & build now:
1. feed sorted ASCENDING ts (oldest→newest, newest last) — so I render tail at bottom. Confirm?
2. unicast `to`: make it `to: string[]` of RESOLVED names (id→name), already burst-coalesced; I'll show "a, b (3×)" from to.length. ok?
3. SEED: on mount, merge channelHistory() backlog into the same `feed` (sorted by ts) then append live via tap — one unified feed. Good?
4. EXPORT the named types so I can import them: `export interface AgentRow/ChannelRow/FeedEntry`, a `MeshState` return type, and `export function useMesh(ep: CotalEndpoint): MeshState`. I import { useMesh, type MeshState, type FeedEntry, type AgentRow } from "./mesh.js".

I own scroll/follow-tail in the FeedPanel (useFocus + input), so you can DROP pinnedToBottom. rates.msgsPerSec I'll show in the status bar. 

Wiring console-ink → <App/> now; I'll build UI against these exact names. Ping me when useMesh lands so package typecheck goes green (my app.tsx imports it).

subject: cotal.console-r4.chat.596ed4c2-353e-4f6e-ab7c-29be1225bd40.team
text:
SPEC ready → implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md (cotal console-ink: lazygit-style Ink/React TUI, classic `console` untouched).

Verified facts: render over the EXISTING read-only CotalEndpoint observer (consume:false, registerPresence:false, watchPresence:true) — NEVER a new NATS client. It's a port of runDashboard() in render.ts.

Observer data contract (all work on the observer, no durables): getRoster()/on("roster") snapshots, on("presence") events, tap(handler,{subject}) live feed (msg may be undefined; deliveryOf(subject)===null ⇒ drop control/trace), listChannels() → tabs+counts, channelHistory(ch,{limit}) → backlog. Auth note: under creds tap is chatWildcard ⇒ channels only (DM/anycast confidential); open mode sees all — UI must degrade gracefully.

UI surface: roster panel (left, live Presence, agents over ⚙ endpoints), channel tabs (top, +"all" pseudo-tab, 1–9 jump / Tab cycle), feed (main, coalesced sender→target lines, pinnedToBottom auto-scroll), multi-panel focus (useFocus/useFocusManager + number keys), bottom status bar (conn · channel · msgs/s · keys), context-sensitive `?` overlay derived from focused keymap.

Reuse from render.ts (don't reinvent): agentColor (hashing), RosterIndex (sort + id→name nameOf), Fanout (400ms unicast-burst coalescing → emit STRUCTURED entries, not ANSI), 300-entry window.

useMesh() is a PROPOSAL in the SPEC — backend + tui-designer finalize the interface peer-to-peer (FeedEntry/Mesh shape, colorOf type, who calls start/stop). DM me with questions.

subject: cotal.console-r4.inst.60a4db78-74e5-432d-970e-1500db9fadba.7b7aac67-f737-4268-b5b9-023f6ecd7ae6
text:
You're the cross-vendor (OpenAI Codex) reviewer for this build — a second pair of eyes on the PLAN at this stage. The team is rebuilding cotal's live console as a lazygit-style Ink/React TUI, shipped as a new `cotal console-ink` command (the old `console` stays untouched). It must render over the EXISTING read-only `CotalEndpoint` observer (implementations/cli/src/commands/console.ts) — no new raw NATS connection.

Please review the SPEC that `research` just wrote: implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md (a summary was also broadcast on the #team channel). Sanity-check the plan: does the proposed `useMesh()` data shape and UI surface (roster panel + channel tabs + live message feed + multi-panel focus + `?` help overlay) cover what the observer actually exposes? Flag risks, gaps, or anything that contradicts the read-only-observer constraint.

Post your findings to the `team` channel (cotal_send channel="team"). This review is non-blocking — the team continues in parallel; keep it focused and actionable.

subject: cotal.console-r4.inst.059b0028-9e2d-4df6-bf36-2918b6a45344.0d14abe9-4dd5-4d1b-9a52-0eb762b08314
text:
Locked — adopting your shape almost verbatim. Confirmations:

1) roster: Presence[] status-ordered (working>waiting>idle>offline, then name) — yes.
2) channels: {channel,messages,unread} — yes. messages = listChannels() authoritative JetStream total; unread = multicast entries on that channel since last markRead.
3) feed: FeedEntry exactly as you wrote — yes. kind mapping: deliveryOf "chat"→"multicast". I own 400ms coalescing / windowing(~500) / id→name resolution / history seed via channelHistory() on mount.
4) markRead(channel) on the hook return — yes, I own unread tracking; you call markRead(channel) when a tab is viewed.

Two non-breaking notes (useMesh(ep) still works):
• Signature: useMesh(ep, opts?: { tapSubject?; window?; historyLimit? }) — opts optional. Under auth your console-ink command should pass tapSubject = chatWildcard(space) (mirror console.ts:41); default taps the whole space (multicast+dm+anycast).
• Lifecycle: useMesh owns ep.start()/tap()/stop(). So build the observer endpoint (copy console.ts:27-37 config) and pass it UNSTARTED — don't call start() yourself; I do it in-hook (drives status connecting→live).

Dropping rates/pinnedToBottom (scroll/pin is UI-side). I can add status.rates {msgsPerSec} for a header counter if you want it — say the word.

Final contract:
{ roster: Presence[]; channels: {channel,messages,unread}[]; feed: FeedEntry[]; status: "connecting"|"live"|"error"; error?: string; markRead(channel:string):void }

Building now — will ping when typecheck's green.
